memory.summary: My roommate's lack of hygiene is ruining my focus and air quality, so I'm leaving for the library immediately to protect my grades. They seem friendly, but this distraction isn't worth ignoring.
The library is my only sanctuary right now; staying home with that roommate risks my GPA, so I'm ignoring the social awkwardness to secure my grades.

desires: Maintain my GPA as the absolute non-negotiable metric of success, viewing any social friction from my roommate as an acceptable tax on that achievement.
Execute a direct, boundary-setting conversation with my roommate immediately to resolve the cleanliness issue without letting it derail my coursework focus.
Avoid feeling exhausted tomorrow by forcing myself to prioritize sleep tonight, despite my current urge to double down on work due to procrastination bias.

goals.description: Send the firm, unambiguous message to roommate_R demanding immediate cleanup or item relocation, then leave the apartment within 5 minutes.
goals.rationale: My achievement drive (0.5) demands an environment conducive to focus; I cannot tolerate distractions while my GPA is at stake. My impulsive nature means I won't wait for them to 'get it'—I will execute this boundary now. Assertiveness dictates a clean break: if they don't move their stuff immediately, I'm leaving and renegotiating later. This is the only way to secure the runway needed for my coursework.

goals.description: Execute the 2-hour deep work block at the library on the reading response draft.
goals.rationale: Once the distraction source is neutralized, my primary metric shifts entirely to outputting this draft by tonight. My short planning horizon means I can't afford to dither; I need to crush this now so I can stop worrying about it and hit other targets before the weekend kills my momentum. Health is secondary to this deadline.

goals.description: Consume a high-protein meal and take a 20-minute power nap before hitting the library.
goals.rationale: I have a sleep debt of 2 hours and my stamina is already dipping. While I want to ignore health (0.13) to get straight to work, crashing tomorrow morning would derail my entire academic runway. A quick refuel is the only way to sustain the aggressive pace I need without burning out before Wednesday.
